content: Modern Monetary Theory (MMT) argues that countries issuing their own sovereign currency cannot involuntarily default on debt denominated in that currency. MMT proponents contend that such governments can always create money to meet obligations, making traditional deficit concerns misplaced. Instead, the real constraint is inflation—government spending should continue until full employment is reached, with taxes used to control inflation rather than fund spending. Critics argue MMT underestimates inflation risks and political constraints on timely tax increases. The theory gained attention during post-2020 fiscal expansions but remains controversial among mainstream economists.
